pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 20 (JOB_NAME="regression-test-suite"), export_result.sh line 9 (JOB_NAME="regression-test-suite"), task.json description explicitly references 'regression-test-suite' job

setup_task.sh lines 69-75 trigger 5 builds sequentially; Jenkins auto-assigns build numbers 1-5. Task description: 'Build #3 has been identified as the stable release candidate.' export_result.sh line 10 sets TARGET_BUILD=3.

task.json description explicitly states: 'Update its Display Name to: RC-v2.1.0'. verifier.py line 27 confirms expected_display_name = 'RC-v2.1.0'. verifier.py lines 73-78 checks for this exact value.

task.json description explicitly states the required description: 'Release Candidate v2.1.0 - All regression tests passed. Approved by QA team for staging deployment.' This text contains all three required snippets. verifier.py lines 86-88 validates each snippet: 'Release Candidate' (line 86), 'v2.1.0' (line 87), 'regression tests passed' (line 88).

Setup script triggers 5 builds (1-5). Target is build 3. Task description states 'Do not modify any other builds.' All other builds are 1, 2, 4, 5. verifier.py lines 98-128 implement anti-gaming checks for these exact build numbers.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'annotate_pin_release_build' requires an agent to locate build #3 of the 'regression-test-suite' job and perform three modifications: (1) update the display name to 'RC-v2.1.0', (2) set the description to text containing 'Release Candidate', 'v2.1.0', and 'regression tests passed', and (3) pin the build by setting keepLog=true. The agent must not modify builds 1, 2, 4, or 5. The task uses a synthetic Jenkins environment with automatically-generated builds created during setup.
